title: Git Internals

concepts.title: Git Objects: Blobs, Trees, and Commits
Branches and HEAD: Pointers, Not Copies
The Staging Area (Index): Git's Waiting Room
Stashing: Temporary Storage for In-Progress Work
Tags and Releases: Marking Important Moments
Remote Repositories: How Push and Pull Work
Performance and Optimization: Git Under Load
Security and Integrity: Trusting Your History
The Future of Git: What's Next?

images.prompt: A retro-terminal-meets-modern-UI cover illustration for a programming book called "Git Internals": a dark navy/charcoal background lit like a CRT terminal, with a glowing electric-blue compass (representing HEAD) at the center, a neon-green tree limb splitting into branching paths behind it, and a small amber camera-shutter icon (representing a commit snapshot) hovering to one side, all connected by thin glowing circuit-like lines suggesting a directed graph of commits. Faint scanlines and a subtle terminal-grid texture in the background. Flat, clean, modern vector illustration style (not photorealistic), high contrast, no text or lettering baked in. Palette: dark navy #0F172A base, neon green #10B981, electric blue #3B82F6, amber #F59E0B accents only. Target size ~1280x720px, 16:9 landscape; compose for a small on-page figure with the subject centred and generous safe margins — it is letterboxed into a 16:9 box, so keep nothing important near the edges.
images.alt: Retro-terminal cover art: a glowing compass (HEAD), a tree limb splitting into branches, and a camera-shutter commit icon over a starfield of green code
images.caption: Cover art slot.

notes: Audience: teens and junior developers. Requested voice: Clear Mentor, very exciting teacher (resolved to persona 'clear-mentor'). Requested look (no matching built-in theme id, design a bespoke skin from this brief): 'Think retro terminal meets modern UI' -- Digital Adventure / Code Quest concept, framing Git as a coding-adventure toolkit. Dark navy/charcoal terminal-like backgrounds with neon green, electric blue, and amber accents. Suggested palette: #0F172A (dark navy), #10B981 (emerald green), #3B82F6 (electric blue), #F59E0B (amber). Suggested fonts: a bold geometric/techy sans for headings (e.g. Orbitron-like), a readable sans for body (e.g. Inter-like), and a monospace for code (e.g. Fira Code-like) -- use local/system font stacks only, no webfonts. Custom icons for Git concepts (branch as tree limb, commit as camera snapshot, HEAD as compass), diagrams color-coded (blobs blue, trees green, commits orange), 'Pro Tip' and 'Warning' callout boxes for dangerous commands (e.g. reset --hard), conversational/mentor tone with light humor, hands-on 'Try It Yourself' labs per chapter. Reference material (for research context only, not to be copied verbatim): Pro Git book ch. 10 Git Internals (git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Git-Internals-Git-Objects); freeCodeCamp 'A Visual Guide to Git Internals'; opensource.com 'Git Concepts in Less Than 10 Minutes'; W3Schools Git intro.
